assumptions.statement: AI data center operators continue seeking long-duration clean power contracts.
Oklo can preserve financing flexibility through the next licensing milestones.

broker_views.thesis: Firm clean power scarcity could support premium economics if Oklo converts interest into binding contracts.

broker_views.target: no target until licensing timeline is dated
broker_views.thesis: Commercial upside remains hard to underwrite before licensing cadence and financing runway are clearer.

catalysts.title: Signed commercial power purchase agreement with credible counterparty.
Material licensing update with a clear review timeline.
Capital raise or partnership that reduces funding risk.

checklist.item: Confirm latest cash balance and expected burn.
Map each announced customer to binding contract evidence.

position_rules.rule: Keep status at watchlist until a dated licensing update is source-backed.
Do not size from AI power narrative without named contract evidence.

positions: Watchlist only until milestone evidence improves.
No thesis action without updated cash runway and licensing review.

reviews.summary: Initial ledger created. Demand backdrop is attractive, but investability depends on licensing cadence, named customer quality, and financing evidence.

reviews.drift: Catalyst tracking added so future reviews separate power-demand validation from Oklo-specific execution evidence.
reviews.summary: Maintain watchlist status until catalysts move from narrative support to dated, source-backed milestones.

risks.mitigation: Require dated regulatory milestones before increasing conviction.
risks.name: Licensing delay

risks.mitigation: Separate power-demand narrative from contracted revenue evidence.
risks.name: Narrative valuation

thesis: Advanced nuclear developers may benefit if AI data center demand increases the value of firm, clean power, but commercialization timing and regulatory execution remain the core constraints.
title: Oklo AI Power Demand Thesis
